title: Migration-induced lock pressure delayed webhook persistence
failure_mechanism: A migration-related lock conflict delayed persistence of delivery events. Consumer processes remained ready but spent more time waiting on database writes, reducing effective throughput.
mitigation_summary: The backlog cleared after database lock pressure subsided and delivery persistence throughput recovered; the historical record is not a recommendation to interrupt a migration.
timeline_summary: A delivery backlog appeared after migration activity began. The consumer fleet remained ready, while database wait signals aligned with lower persistence throughput.
narrative_safe: RelayOps webhook delivery consumers were healthy but slow after migration activity began. Queue growth alone is insufficient to classify this as a consumer failure; migration lock waits must be checked first.
